mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE stays below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts signals a synchronized contraction in consumer and housing demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth remains above 2.0% annualized, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain normalization, a monthly increase in industrial production above 0.5% signals robust manufacturing output that boosts short-term equity momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during inflationary supply shocks, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative, prompting equity investors to rotate into growth assets and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative real rates coincide with negative equity returns when central banks signal aggressive future hikes, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate consumer spending capacity, which lifts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns when wage inflation exceeds 5.0% annually, or the 60-day return distribution shows no skew toward positive outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 1.0% annualized, weak economic momentum triggers earnings downgrade cycles that immediately suppress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The market prices in the slowdown ahead of the data release, causing positive returns upon publication, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while unemployment falls signals a synchronized economic acceleration that boosts short-term risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails during stagflationary periods where supply constraints dominate, or the joint movement shows no predictive power for 5-day returns in walk-forward tests.
